// Operational Briefing

SOME PROJECTS & Case Studies

CVE-RSS Alert System

Problem Statement

"Security teams spend 4+ hours weekly manually checking NVD/CISA databases, risking missed 'Zero-Day' threats."

Operational Impact

95% reduction in manual monitoring time. 60% reduction in alert noise via CVSS logic-based filtering.

Environment / Stack

n8n, NVD API, CISA Feed

Access Technical Briefing _

Secret Exposure Pipeline

Problem Statement

"Accidental credential commits in public repos create immediate entry points for attackers."

Operational Impact

Real-time detection of 15+ secret types. Continuous monitoring of attack surface with 0ms manual oversight.

Environment / Stack

Regex, GitHub API, n8n

Access Technical Briefing _

OSINT Email Harvester

Problem Statement

"Manual reconnaissance for phishing simulations is time-expensive and prone to data errors."

Operational Impact

Reduced recon phase from hours to seconds. Improved simulation accuracy by 40% via automated validation.

Environment / Stack

Regex, RFC 5322, n8n

Access Technical Briefing _

Security is not about the absence of risk, but the resilience to survive it.

I reject the idea that security must hinder usability. True infrastructure security should be transparent, protecting the user without obstructing the workflow.

Investing in security is not a diversion of resources; it is a preservation of value. Robust defense ensures that innovation is not erased by a single point of failure.

// Featured Architecture

Case Study: CVE-RSS Alert System

01

Problem Analysis

Security teams face "Alert Fatigue" from manual monitoring of NVD and CISA databases. Manually parsing RSS feeds results in a 4-6 hour delay between vulnerability publication and internal triage, leaving a critical window open for exploitation.

Slow Response Manual Triage
02

Solution Design

I designed an event-driven pipeline using n8n to automate the entire lifecycle of a vulnerability. The system architecture includes:

  • → CISA/NVD Ingestion: Real-time polling via RSS and REST API.
  • → State Management: Google Sheets acts as a lightweight DB to track "Seen" CVEs and prevent alert duplication.
  • → Dynamic Risk Scoring: Custom JS node to parse multi-version CVSS (3.1, 3.0, 2.0) and route by severity.

The Stack

n8nNVD API v2.0RegexJavascriptGoogle SheetsGmail

Impact Metrics

  • + 95% Reduction in Triage Time
  • + Zero Duplicated Alerts
  • + Instant Critical Notification
Logic: Risk_Scoring.js 
// Extracted from n8n Workflow
if (score >= 9) {
  severity = "CRITICAL";
  decision = "alert"; // Instant Gmail
} else if (score >= 7.5) {
  severity = "HIGH";
  decision = "review"; // Manual Check
} else {
  severity = "LOW/MED";
  decision = "log"; // Audit Only
}

Lessons Learned

The biggest challenge was handling the NVD API rate limits and data inconsistency between CVSS versions. I optimized the workflow by implementing a Lookup node that only queries the API if the CVE is not already in the audit log, saving hundreds of unnecessary API calls daily.
